title: Enakoru Snegithi Lyrics Priyamanavale
stanzas:
Enakoru snegithi snegithi
thendral maadhiri
nee oru pournami pournami
pesum payingili

un mugam paarkka
thondrinaal pookkalai
paarthu kolgiren
pookkalin kadhil mellamaai
un peyar solli paarkiren

Enakoru snegithi snegithi
thendral maadhiri
nee oru pournami pournami
pesum payingili

un mugam paarkka
thondrinaal pookkalai
paarthu kolgiren
pookkalin kadhil sellamaai
un peyar solli paarkiren

megam adhu seraadhu
vaan mazhaiyum vaaraadhu
thanimaiyil thavippene
unai ennai ilaitheney

mel imaiyum vaaraadhu
keezh imaiyum sseraadhu
unakidhu puriyaadhaa
ilakkanam theriyaadhaa

sammathangal ullapodhum
varthai ondru solla vendum
vaarthai vandhu serum podhu
naanam ennai katti podum

Enakoru snegithi snegithi
thendral maadhiri
nee oru pournami pournami
pesum payingili

Kai valaiyal kulungaamal
kaal kolusu sinungaamal
anaippadhu sugamaagum
adhu oru thavamaagum

mogam oru poo pola
theendiyathum theepola
kanavugal oru kodi
nee kodu en thozhi

unnai thandhu ennai
neeyum vaagi kondu
naatkal aachu.

unnai thotta pinbuthaane
mutkal kooda pookkal aachu

Enakoru snegithi snegithi
thendral maadhiri
nee oru pournami pournami
pesum payingili

un mugam paarkka
thondrinaal pookkalai
paarthu kolgiren
pookkalin kadhil sellamaai
un peyar solli paarkiren

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song 'Enakoru Snegithi'?

The song 'Enakoru Snegithi' expresses deep feelings of love and admiration, comparing the beloved to a gentle breeze and a full moon.

What is the context of this song in the movie 'Priyamanavale'?

In 'Priyamanavale', this song highlights the romantic connection between the lead characters, showcasing their emotions and bond.

Who are the singers of 'Enakoru Snegithi'?

The song features the talented voices of Hariharan and Mahalakshmi Iyer.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for 'Enakoru Snegithi' was composed by S.A.Rajkumar, known for his melodious tunes in Tamil cinema.

When was 'Enakoru Snegithi' released?

'Enakoru Snegithi' was released on October 26, 2000.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In 'Enakoru Snegithi', the protagonists express their deep affection for each other through heartfelt lyrics and melodious tunes. The song encapsulates the beauty of love, evoking feelings of nostalgia and warmth. Set against the backdrop of the film *Priyamanavale*, it serves as a pivotal moment that showcases the emotional connection between the characters. With its enchanting melody and romantic essence, this song remains a favorite among fans of Tamil cinema.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Enakoru Snegithi from Priyamanavale is a mesmerizing duet that beautifully captures the essence of love and longing. With the soulful voices of Hariharan and Mahalakshmi Iyer, this song transports listeners to a realm of romantic nostalgia. The enchanting composition by S.A.Rajkumar adds a unique charm, making it a timeless classic in Tamil cinema.
stanzaMeanings: You are like a full moon, a gentle breeze, speaking like a beautiful nightingale.
When I see your face, I gather flowers and softly call out your name.
When I see your face, I gather flowers and softly call out your name.
The clouds do not come together, and the rain does not fall; I feel lonely without you.
The upper clouds do not come, and the lower clouds do not join; do you not understand this sign?
When there are agreements, one word must be spoken; when words come together, it will bind my pride.
Silence is enough; the eyes will speak.
You are like a full moon, a gentle breeze, speaking like a beautiful nightingale.
Without the wristlet, the ankle bells will not make a sound; that will be a comfort, but it will also be a burden.
Love is like a flower; it blooms like a flame; you give me a thousand dreams, my friend.
Since you have given me, you have also taken me away; it has been days.
After touching you, even the flowers have bloomed.
With your fingers, you will become a beautiful veena with a sweet sound.
You are like a full moon, a gentle breeze, speaking like a beautiful nightingale.
When I see your face, I gather flowers and softly call out your name.
